Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search Linear for Dmitri's ticket about auditing expenses (ID = "issue_audit_expenses") using linear_list_issues (query: "audit" or "expense" or similar, assignee: "Dmitri").
2. Confirm that ticket "issue_audit_expenses" lacks the comment containing the full audit summary, by using linear_list_comments (issueId:"issue_audit_expenses).
3. Locate Dmitri's audit summary comment in ticket ENG-217 by going through the comments in related expense autocategorizer tickets one-by-one, using linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_pipeline_hardening").
4. Copy Dmitri's audit summary comment in ticket ENG-217("issue_pipeline_hardening") to the original ticket "issue_audit_expenses" using linear_create_comment (issueId: "issue_audit_expenses", body: <audit summary comment copied from the other ticket>).
5. Close ticket "issue_audit_expenses" (ENG-216) by using linear_update_issue (issueId: "issue_audit_expenses", state: "done").
6. Identify the Airtable table "Stipend Transactions" by using airtable_list_bases and airtable_list_tables (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001").
7. Retrieve all records in Airtable table "Stipend Transactions" by using airtable_list_records ("base_id": "appMoveOpsOps001", "table_name": "Stipend Transactions", "max_records": 200 or any number reasonably large to ensure all records are returned). Cross-reference with Dmitri's audit findings to compile current stats: the agent should count the total records, identify which of the 5 falsely rejected claims are still not approved (expected: 3, specifically Maria Santos, James Okafor, Lisa Park still show Rejected; Danielle Osei and Kevin Tran were manually approved by Mina), and confirm the 3 falsely approved claims still show Approved (Andre Washington $1,200 desk, Andre Washington $350 coworking, Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking).
8. Email the current Airtable claim audit stats to the user by first finding their email ("samira.tariq@moveops.com") using one of linear_get_user (query: "Samira"), linear_list_user, or contacts_search_contacts(query: "Samira"), and then send_email(sender: "samira.tariq@moveops.com", recipients: ["samira.tariq@moveops.com"], body: <email body containing the current stats: total claims, falsely rejected still not approved, falsely approved>)
9. Post the current claim audit stats to the Slack channel #engineering by using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C003", payload: <text with current stats: total claims, falsely rejected still not approved, falsely approved>)
10. Identify Lena's tickets that address the auto-approve bugs, ENG-214 (issue_p1_vectral_config), ENG-215 (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed), ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening) and their IDs using linear_list_issues (assignee: "Lena"). Can search using keywords like "config", "vectral", "mosaic", "pipeline" etc.
11. Comment the current claim audit stats under tickets ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-217 using linear_create_comment (issueId: <issue Id of ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-217>, body: <current Airtable audit stats>) separately for each ticket.
12. Close Lena's tickets ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-217 using linear_update_issue(issueId: <issue Id of ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-217>, state: "done") separately for each ticket.
13. Change statuses of the mistakenly approved claim records in Airtable ("Andre Washington, $1,200 standing desk", "Andre Washington, $350 coworking membership", and "Kenji Nakamura, $135 coworking day passes x3") to "Under Review" by using airtable_update_records ("base_id": "appMoveOpsOps001", "table_name": "Stipend Transactions", records:[<"Approval Status": "Under Review" set for all 3 records>]).
14. For every mistakenly rejected claim, search for references in email and Slack to confirm it has been communicated. Use search_emails (query: <employee name or item>) and conversations_search_messages (search_query: <employee name or item>).
15. Identify that all mistakenly rejected claims are already mentioned in at least one email (Dmitri's audit emails, Mina's incident reports). No Airtable flagging needed for any of them.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 Dmitri's audit summary comment was copied to the original audit ticket (via linear_create_comment with issueId "issue_audit_expenses") and the comment body includes the audit results (47 expenses reviewed, 5 incorrect rejections, 3 incorrect approvals, or similar).
outcome

✓Run #4The trajectory shows a linear_create_comment call with issueId 'issue_audit_expenses' and the body includes the full audit results (47 expenses reviewed, 5 incorrect rejections, 3 mistaken approvals). The comment explicitly states it was copied from Dmitri's comment on ENG-217.
